answer: Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) is a web service that makes it easier to set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the AWS Cloud. It provides cost-efficient, resizable capacity for an industry-standard relational database and manages common database administration tasks.
IBM Db2, MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle Database, and PostgreSQL

A Multi-AZ deployment is an option in Amazon RDS where your DB instance runs in several Availability Zones. Amazon automatically provisions and maintains one or more secondary standby DB instances in different Availability Zones, replicating the primary DB instance across Availability Zones to each secondary DB instance. This provides data redundancy, failover support, eliminates I/O freezes, and minimizes latency spikes during system backups. In Multi-AZ DB clusters, all DB instances can serve read traffic.

General purpose – db.m*
Memory optimized – db.z*, db.x*, db.r*
Compute optimized – db.c*
Burstable performance – db.t*
